settings.title: Greystone Opens a Dedicated Employment Law Practice
settings.excerpt: Local businesses now have a single point of contact for contracts, disputes, and workplace policy.
settings.body:
We have advised on employment matters for years as part of our wider work. From this month, Greystone has a dedicated employment law team so clients no longer have to piece that advice together across departments.

The practice covers the day-to-day questions that come up for any employer. That means contracts, handbooks, disciplinary processes, redundancy, and the disputes that occasionally follow.

Why we set it up

Employment questions rarely arrive neatly. An owner calls about one contract and, in passing, mentions a grievance, a sickness issue, and a worry about a new hire. Handing those across separate departments slowed everything down and left clients repeating themselves. A single team that holds the whole picture is simply faster and clearer.

The team handles the matters that land on an employer's desk most often.

  • Contracts of employment and staff handbooks
  • Disciplinary and grievance procedures
  • Redundancy and restructuring
  • Disputes and tribunal claims

Who leads it

It is led by Priya Anand, who has spent fifteen years on both the employer and employee side of the table. That mix matters. Having argued cases from both directions, she tends to see where a dispute is heading well before it gets there, which is exactly when good advice is worth most.

Existing clients can reach the team through their usual Greystone contact. There is nothing you need to do differently. The advice you were already getting is now joined up and sitting in one place.
